I have been using `qwen-3.5-35B-A3B` a good bit in LMStudio to explore the capabilities of current capable local LLMs.  In this post, I recount a conversation with the local installation of qwen about YDataProfiling, a python package for exploratory data analysis.

# System Instruction


# Asking qwen


::: {.callout-caution}
## Prompt

Write up a brief summary set of commands for using YDataProfiling in python.  Construct some code to import this csv of data into python and examine it.  The data is one cross-section of funds so time series capabilities will be of minimal interest here but should be documented nevertheless  [a link to the data is provided](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/robertwwalker/DADMStuff/master/BondFunds.csv)
:::


Based on my research, here's a complete summary of YDataProfiling commands and working code for your BondFunds.csv data:

---

# YDataProfiling Command Summary

## Installation

```
pip install ydata-profiling
# or with conda
conda install -c conda-forge ydata-profiling
```

## Basic Usage Commands

| Function | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| `ProfileReport()` | Creates a profiling report from a DataFrame | `profile = ProfileReport(df, title="My Report")` |
| `.to_file()` | Exports report to HTML or JSON file | `profile.to_file("report.html")` |
| `.to_notebook_iframe()` | Displays inline in Jupyter Notebook | `profile.to_notebook_iframe()` |
| `compare()` | Compares two datasets side-by-side | `ProfileReport(df1).compare(ProfileReport(df2))` |

## Key Configuration Options

```
ProfileReport(
    df,                           # pandas DataFrame to profile
    title="Report Title",         # Report heading
    tsmode=False,                 # Enable time-series mode
    sortby=None,                  # Column name for chronological order (tsmode=True)
    samples={"head": 10},         # Data samples shown in report
    correlations={"pearson": True}   # Correlation matrix options
)
```

## Time Series Configuration

```
# Enable time-series analysis mode
profile = ProfileReport(
    df, 
    tsmode=True,                # Required for time-series profiling
    sortby="date_column_name"   # Column containing chronological data
)
```

---

# Working Code: BondFunds.csv Analysis


::: {.cell}

```{.python .cell-code}
import pandas as pd
from ydata_profiling import ProfileReport

# ============================================
# 1. Load the Data
# ============================================
url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/robertwwalker/DADMStuff/master/BondFunds.csv"
df_bondfunds = pd.read_csv(url)

print(f"Dataset shape: {df_bondfunds.shape}")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Dataset shape: (184, 9)
```


:::

```{.python .cell-code}
print(f"\nColumn names:\n{list(df_bondfunds.columns)}")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

['Fund Number', 'Type', 'Assets', 'Fees', 'Expense Ratio', 'Return 2009', '3-Year Return', '5-Year Return', 'Risk']
```


```

```

:::

```{.python .cell-code}
# ============================================
# 2. Basic Profile Report (Cross-Section Focus)
# ============================================
profile_basic = ProfileReport(
    df_bondfunds, 
    title="Bond Funds - Cross-Section Analysis"
)

# Generate and save the HTML report
profile_basic.to_file("img/bond_funds_report.html")
```

```{.python .cell-code}
    print(f"  Mean: {df_bondfunds[col].mean():.2f}")
```

---

## Output Files Generated

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| [`bond_funds_report.html`](https://robertwwalker.github.io/conversations-with-qwen/YData/img/bond_funds_report.html) | Interactive HTML report with visualizations |
| `bond_funds_metrics.json` | Machine-readable metrics for programmatic access |


```{=html}
<iframe width="60%" height="50%" src="https://robertwwalker.github.io/conversations-with-qwen/posts/YData/img/bond_funds_report.html" title="Report"></iframe>
```

---

## Key Observations for BondFunds Dataset

Based on the CSV structure, this dataset contains:

- **Fund identifiers** (FN-1 through FN-43+)   
- **Fund types** (Intermediate Government)   
- **Assets** (in millions USD)   
- **Fee information** (No/Yes binary)   
- **Expense ratios** (decimal values)   
- **Returns** across multiple time periods (2009, 3-Year, 5-Year)   
- **Risk ratings** (Below/Average/Above average)   

The HTML report will provide:

1. Variable type classification   
2. Missing value analysis   
3. Correlation matrices   
4. Distribution plots for numeric variables   
5. Value counts for categorical variables   
6. Data quality warnings
